## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hi, I'm Mark Evans, one of your PLF coaches, and in this bonus, I'm going to share with you how to build your list during a launch with Facebook ads. This is going to be behind the scenes of how we build our list during a launch with Facebook ads. Now, it's important to know this is a more advanced topic, and it'll be valuable to you if, number one, you already know that your audience is on Facebook. Number two, you have your offer ready to drive people to. Number three, you have a budget for paid ads. And number four, you're willing to take the time to learn Facebook ads. If you're just getting started, you should learn Facebook ads yourself. This isn't something that we're going to teach here in PLF. We're not going to get into how to create your ads account, how to create an ad to start this advertising. What we do recommend is that you get your training from Facebook itself. No one wants you to succeed more in Facebook advertising than Facebook themselves, because obviously you'll be spending money with them. So they have some of the best training out there to learn how to do Facebook ads. So I highly recommend learning the training in the beginning, definitely straight from Facebook. And, you know, in the beginning, it's just the best way to learn how this whole process works is by doing it yourself. So if you have the time and the bandwidth, I do recommend doing it yourself. So another alternative if you're getting started is you could always have somebody on your team learn it. That's another option for you. If you do have a team at this point to get somebody else to learn it and bring it in house for your team is highly recommended. And then at some point down the road, you definitely might want to think about hiring an expert that can take over your advertising needs for your business. So you can outsource that and worry about some of the other strategic things inside your company while your Facebook advertising is being taken care of by third party. So now I want to get started and take you behind the scenes and show you step by step how we run the Facebook ad campaigns during our launch. So this is broken up into three phases. The first phase is our lead generation phase. Our second phase is our engagement phase. And our third phase is our sales phase. Now during our lead generation phase, we're starting out by getting leads into our funnel. And it's a combination of different ways that we go out to get it. And the way that we do it is both from an internal angle and an external angle. Internally we're using our own subscriber list, which we upload to Facebook and we create a customized audience out of it. We also take our unsubscribe lists, people that have unsubscribed from our mailing list. We actually upload that list to Facebook as well because these are people that we could put a Facebook ad in front of and they might want to reengage with us. And actually we found that many of them do.

## [03:02 - 06:02]

They might have dropped off our list over the past year or a couple of years and they now want to reengage and get involved with our launch process. So we upload that as a customized audience also. We also target people that have been engaged with our list more frequently in the past 180 days specifically. We also target our fans of all of our pages and all the visitors that have come to our website. So those are the main areas internally that we start focusing on for lead generation before we start our launch process for a big JV launch. Now externally we go to different types of markets and externally we do this for Evergreen also but we definitely target as you would assume other people in the internet marketing space. We target people tools actually. So we target like email hosting companies like Aweber or Infusionsoft or people that are using tools that are in our industry and if they have big followings we actually target them on Facebook as well externally. Service providers is the word that I'm looking for. And then what we do is we create lookalike audiences from them once we start capturing those audiences so that Facebook can do the heavy lifting they can go out and find people that are similar to those people that we're targeting. And we create lookalike audiences for our internal also so we can get a bigger list of people to come in. So that's mostly the internal and the external that we target during our lead generation part and our placement. So we're placing them on the Facebook network, the audience network, Instagram for those that allow the Instagram ads to go on there depending on their parameters. ## [15:08 - 18:12]

And I think we have about 11 different case studies that we use. And we tested different people because we have 11 different people that have different case studies here. And we started seeing what results were we getting in with conversion. So as you can see here, this is not a video ad anymore, but it does go to a page where Cami's video is talking about her case study and using PLF in the author and artist market. So we have now switched over from a video ad to a picture ad. And this has been really successful for us. And if you have case studies that you're using in your launch process, I highly recommend doing some testing with some different people and see what your market is resonating with to see if that moves the needle with some conversions. But it's been super helpful for us. All right, then as we're starting to wind down our open cart, of course, we have the scarcity segments where our emails go all into the scarcity that we're about to close our cart and you have a limited amount of time to go in and get involved in product launch formula. So we do the same process on Facebook. And it goes in a couple of different directions. For Rook, use a couple of different ads that have worked real well. This first one is a video talking about how there's only 48 hours left and the scarcity of that and that we're starting our new class that following week and we're closing down tomorrow. So this is Jeff again, talking for about 30 seconds with that scarcity. And this type of scarcity, we're doing an email also. This is just another avenue for us to do that. And then for Rook has found, not just with us, but with other clients, putting these kind of picture ads, they're not video ads, but they're picture ads showing that there's only 48 hours left seem to be converting really well. So he does it both with a 48 hour counter and then with a 24 hour counter. And this is just a picture ad that's getting more people involved into the scarcity. And we space these ads out over the 48 hour period so people have an opportunity to see both ads coming through. And then the last ad we used is the 24 hour one. And the 24 hour one is, it's a picture ad, but it's like one of those flash videos that like counts down from 24 down to zero, but it does it really quick. So it's one of those like, you know, flash countdown ads, but it converts pretty good.
